question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted transaction targeting a contract address that is NOT in the `contract` Set, causing `contractFilterCheck()` to return false and allowing full trace logging of sensitive calldata (input, output, return) that would otherwise be filtered, leading to unintended information disclosure of transaction internals?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged user craft a transaction with a recipient address that, after EVM address normalization via `CommonUtils.hex(toEvmAddress(...))`, produces a string that does NOT match any entry in the `contract` Set even though the logical contract is filtered, bypassing `contractFilterCheck()` and causing sensitive transaction trace data to be logged?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction that deliberately triggers a specific `MessageFrame.State` (e.g., REVERT) not present in the `status` Set, causing `stateFilterCheck()` to return false and bypassing the state filter so that the full transaction trace including revert reason and input data is logged?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged user craft a transaction that forces the EVM frame into a `State` that is absent from the `status` Set, causing `stateFilterCheck()` to return false and allowing the trace log to emit the full `input=`, `output=`, and `return=` fields of the tampered transaction, leaking sensitive ABI-encoded calldata?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction with an empty `contract` Set precondition (i.e., when `getContract().isEmpty()` is true), causing `contractFilterCheck()` to always return false regardless of the recipient address, meaning every transaction trace is logged and the attacker can observe all other users' transaction input/output data through log aggregation?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the condition `!getStatus().isEmpty() && !getStatus().contains(state)` in `stateFilterCheck()` by submitting transactions that produce EVM states not in the configured `status` Set, effectively bypassing the filter and causing the tracer to log the full transaction execution trace including sensitive calldata from the tampered transaction?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged user inject log-forging payloads into the `input` field of a submitted transaction such that when `contractFilterCheck()` returns false and the tracer logs `input=<hex>`, the hex-encoded calldata, when decoded, contains newline or control characters that corrupt structured log output and tamper with audit trail integrity?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction to a contract address with all-zero padding (e.g., `0x0000...000X`) that passes `@Hex(minLength=40, maxLength=40)` validation but whose normalized EVM address form differs from the stored filter entry, causing `contractFilterCheck()` to fail to filter it and logging the full transaction trace?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged user exploit case-sensitivity differences between the hex address stored in the `contract` Set and the address produced by `CommonUtils.hex(toEvmAddress(...))` to bypass `contractFilterCheck()`, causing the filter to miss the contract and log the full trace of a tampered transaction?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged user craft a transaction that causes the EVM frame to enter `State.CODE_EXECUTING` while the `status` Set contains only `State.COMPLETED_SUCCESS`, causing `stateFilterCheck()` to return true and silently suppress the trace, allowing the attacker to hide evidence of a malicious transaction from the audit log?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction where the recipient address resolves to `Optional.empty()` via `commonEntityAccessor.get()`, causing the `contractFilterCheck()` branch to be skipped entirely and the full trace to be logged regardless of the `contract` filter Set, leaking transaction data?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction targeting a non-existent contract address (one that returns `Optional.empty()` from `commonEntityAccessor`), bypassing the `contractFilterCheck()` guard entirely and causing the tracer to log the full transaction trace including the tampered calldata?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged user submit a high-volume stream of transactions that each produce a `State` not in the `status` Set, causing `stateFilterCheck()` to return false for all of them and flooding the trace log with entries, leading to log storage exhaustion and denial of service for the monitoring/audit infrastructure?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged user submit a massive number of transactions to contracts not in the `contract` Set, causing `contractFilterCheck()` to return false for all of them and triggering unbounded `log.info()` calls in `tracePostExecution()`, exhausting log buffer memory and causing a denial-of-service condition?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged user craft a transaction with a `from` address that, when logged as `sender=` in the trace, contains a hex string that mimics a legitimate operator address, causing log-based monitoring systems to misattribute the tampered transaction to a trusted party?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the boolean logic `!getStatus().isEmpty() && !getStatus().contains(state)` in `stateFilterCheck()` by submitting transactions that always produce states outside the configured filter, effectively making the state filter a no-op and ensuring all transaction traces are logged, exposing other users' transaction data?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction with a `to` address that is a valid 40-char hex string passing `@Hex` validation but represents a precompile address not tracked by `commonEntityAccessor`, causing `contractFilterCheck()` to be skipped and the full precompile interaction trace (including sensitive input/output) to be logged?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction with crafted ABI-encoded calldata in the `data` field that, when logged as `input=<hex>` after `contractFilterCheck()` passes, encodes a CRLF injection sequence that splits the log entry and inserts a fake log line, tampering with the audit trail?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction that deliberately causes a `REVERT` state (not in the `status` Set), causing `stateFilterCheck()` to return false and the tracer to log the `revertReason=` field, potentially leaking sensitive error messages or internal state information from the contract?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that `contractFilterCheck()` compares against the hex-encoded EVM address (via `CommonUtils.hex(toEvmAddress(entity.getId()))`) rather than the raw submitted address, by submitting a transaction to an alias address that maps to a different entity ID, causing the filter to miss the contract and log the full trace?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction where `frame.getRecipientAddress()` returns null (e.g., contract creation), causing the null-check branch in `tracePostExecution()` to set `recipientNum` to `Optional.empty()` and skip `contractFilterCheck()`, resulting in the full contract creation trace being logged including the deployment bytecode?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction that transitions through multiple EVM states, where only some states are in the `status` Set, causing `stateFilterCheck()` to selectively log partial execution traces that reveal intermediate contract state without logging the final outcome, creating an inconsistent audit record?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction with a very large `data` payload that passes hex validation, and when `contractFilterCheck()` returns false causing the tracer to log `input=<large_hex>`, the log entry size causes log system buffer overflow or truncation, corrupting adjacent log entries and tampering with the audit trail?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction that causes `State.EXCEPTIONAL_HALT`, which is not in the `status` Set, causing `stateFilterCheck()` to return false and the tracer to log the full execution trace including the `remainingGas=` value, leaking gas consumption patterns that reveal internal contract logic?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `HashSet` implementation of the `contract` field by submitting transactions to contracts whose addresses, after normalization, produce hash collisions in the `HashSet`, causing `contractFilterCheck()` to incorrectly return false and log traces for contracts that should be filtered?
